LAW AND MOTION 302, A R G U E D; PLAINTIFF RUSSELL BRIMER'S MOTION TO APPROVE PROPOSITION 65 SETTLEMENT AND ENTER PROPOSED JUDGMENT IS CONTINUED TO APRIL 2, 2012 AND GRANTED IN PART. ALL PORTIONS OF THE SETTLEMENT ARE APPROVED INCLUDING THE TOTAL AMOUNT OF THE PAYMENT TO BE MADE BY DEFENDANT EXCEPT THE ALLOCATION OF THAT PAYMENT BETWEEN A CIVIL PENALTY AND ATTORNEY'S FEES. THE ISSUE OF THE ALLOCATION OF THE PAYMENT IS CONTINUED TO APRIL 2, 2012. PLAINTIFF IS REQUESTED TO FILE SUPPLEMENTAL PAPERS NO LATER THAN MARCH 27, 2012 INCLUDING CONTEMPORANEOUS BILLING RECORDS AND INFORMATION REGARDING EACH TIMEKEEPER AS WELL AS AN EXPLANATION HOW THE ALLOCATION WAS MADE AND WHY THAT ALLOCATION IS FAIR AND REASONABLE. THE COURT WILL CONTINUE THE TRIAL DATE IN THIS CASE AND THE PARTIES WILL INFORM THE COURT OF THE DATE BY WAY OF E-MAIL. JUDGE: HAROLD E. KAHN; COURT REPORTER: LAVENA WARD CSR #7077

DISCOVERY 301, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: PLAINTIFF RUSSELL BRIMER'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO WRITTEN DISCOVERY AND FOR SANCTIONS AGAINST DEFENDANT AND THEIR COUNSEL IS GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. DEFENDANT MUST PROVIDE FURTHER RESPONSES TO INTERROGATORIES NO. 20 AND REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION NOS. 15 AND 20 AND, TO THE EXTENT THERE IS ADDITIONAL INFORMATION, MUST SUPPLEMENT ALL RESPONSES TO COVER THE TIME PERIOD BEGINNING WITH THE YEAR 2008, TO INCLUDE ALL COLORED, VINYL-COATED PAPER FASTENERS, AND TO IDENTIFY ANY INFORMATION OR DOCUMENTS WITHHELD ON THE GROUND OF PRIVILEGE, PROVIDING A LOG SUFFICIENT TO PERMIT PLAINTIFF TO CONTEST ANY SUCH CLAIM OF PRIVILEGE. THE COURT DID NOT AWARD SANCTIONS. PREVAILING PARTY IS TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, NOT REPORTED.

Notice Of Motion To Approve Prop 65 Settlement And Enter Proposed Judgment
Off Calendar Mar-14-2012 Continued To Apr-02-12 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES - ATTY. GREGORY M. SHEFFER OF THE SHEFFER LAW FIRM, TEL. NO. 388-0911 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. WILLIAM TROUTMAN, APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT OFFICE DEPOT BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE.

RULING - A R G U E D; PLAINTIFF RUSSELL BRIMER'S Motion To Approve Proposition 65 Settlement And Enter Proposed Judgment is continued to April 2, 2012 and Granted in part. All portions of the settlement are approved including the total amount of the payment to be made by defendant except the allocation of that payment between a civil penalty and attorney's fees. The issue of the allocation of the payment is continued to April 2, 2012. Plaintiff is requested to file supplemental papers no later than March 27, 2012 including contemporaneous billing records and information regarding each timekeeper as well as an explanation how the allocation was made and why that allocation is fair and reasonable. The court will continue the trial date in this case and the parties will inform the court of the date by way of e-mail. Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Court Reporter: LaVena Ward CSR #7077 =302/HK
